morphology.summary: A Latinate form connected to root-related meaning plus an adjectival suffix.

etymology.summary: The lexical family is connected to Latin radix, meaning root, which supports later senses of fundamental or root-level change.

semantic_drift.summary: The word moves from root-related meaning to fundamental, then to root-level reform and later to political or social extremity in some contexts.

developments.to_sense: basic, fundamental, or reaching the root
developments.description: Root imagery supports the abstract sense of getting to the foundation of a matter.

developments.to_sense: advocating deep social or political change
developments.description: The idea of root-level change becomes associated with reform movements and political programs.
The reformist sense broadens into evaluative uses that may be admiring, alarming, or casual.

present_usage.summary: Modern meanings include root-level, politically reformist, extreme, and informal positive senses.

senses.definition: Relating to the root, basis, or fundamental nature of something.

examples.text: The report called for a radical redesign of the system.
examples.note: Seed usage example created for the MVP.

senses.definition: Favoring deep or far-reaching change in social or political arrangements.

examples.text: Opponents described the proposal as radical.
examples.note: Seed usage example created for the MVP.
Seed usage example created for the MVP.

cultural_frame.summary: The word can invoke scholarship, technical precision, reform politics, danger, youth slang, or countercultural style.

frames.label: root-cause analysis
frames.description: Frames action as addressing foundations rather than surface symptoms.

frames.label: political threat or reform
frames.description: Frames change as deep enough to be either necessary reform or dangerous disruption.

frames.label: informal admiration
frames.description: Frames an act, object, or person as strikingly impressive.

pragmatics.summary: Radical can be neutral, admiring, alarming, dismissive, or casually approving depending on discourse frame.

stances.label: technical precision
stances.description: Marks an analysis or intervention as reaching a root or foundation.

stances.label: warning
stances.description: Marks an idea or group as too extreme or destabilizing.

stances.label: praise
stances.description: Praises ambition, originality, or courage to change the foundations.

stances.label: slang approval
stances.description: Uses the word informally to mean excellent or impressive.

expansion.paragraph: Radical should not be reduced to extreme. Its root imagery supports meanings around foundations, root causes, and deep change. In political discourse, that root-level sense can frame reform as necessary transformation or as dangerous disruption. In informal usage, the word can also become a term of admiration. Context decides whether radical is technical, threatening, inspiring, or slang praise.

trace.contribution: The Latin root frame supplies the foundation-oriented meaning.
Root-level meaning extends to deep reform and then to extremity or intensity.
The word can warn, praise, analyze, or signal informal approval.

capsule_summary: A root-derived word whose modern meanings range from fundamental to politically transformative, extreme, or informally excellent.

relations.note: Best fit for root-level or basis-oriented contexts.
Only appropriate in evaluative or political contexts.

provenance.citation: Etymonoetic Interlingua MVP seed capsule for radical.
provenance.note: Demonstrates schema shape. Replace or supplement with cited lexicographic and corpus sources before production use.

unresolved_questions: Add cited dictionary and corpus evidence for each sense.
Split technical mathematical, chemical, medical, and political senses more finely in a production capsule.
